name: MAVEN
first: First mission dedicated to studying how Mars lost its atmosphere
description: MAVEN — Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution — answers the question 'where did Mars's air go?' Its elliptical orbit dips into the upper atmosphere, sampling ions and neutrals as the solar wind strips them away. Findings: Mars loses about 100 g of atmosphere per second to space — over billions of years that's enough to explain the missing climate. The spacecraft also serves as a critical relay for surface assets, including Curiosity and Perseverance.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Atlas V 401 from Cape Canaveral SLC-41.

events.label: MARS ORBIT INSERTION
events.note: 33-minute main engine burn captures MAVEN into a 35-hour elliptical science orbit.

events.label: FIRST SCIENCE
events.note: Begins atmosphere-sampling 'deep dip' campaigns to periapsis altitudes ~125 km.
